name: MCP reliability and failure modes
summary: Infrastructure failures distinguish MCP from CLI in production harnesses. Scalekit reports 28% MCP run failures (7/25) due to ConnectTimeout to remote GitHub Copilot MCP, while CLI completed 25/25 runs. Task-level success when connected was high for both. Separate from token optimization: GitHub cli-proxy reports ~24% workflow token reduction by removing MCP schemas in an agent loop—that is client architecture, not the 32x per-task gap.
methodology_notes: Scalekit n=25 per modality subset within 75 total runs. cli-proxy figure from GitHub gh-aw-firewall issue #1885 (workflow-level, n=1 style post). CLI Agent Spec catalogs structured exit codes and retry semantics for agent-native CLIs.
implications_for_gax: gaxd as local broker reduces remote MCP connection churn for agents. Envelope error kinds (adapter_error, policy_denied) support CLI Agent Spec-style automation. Eval models MCP reliability at 0.72 for composite scoring until live MCP runs are added.
corrections_to_repo_docs: research/01: clarify 28% is run/infrastructure failure, not task logic failure when connected.
